divergences.decision: System context and deployment are modeled around worker/master coordination plus ETCD or built-in Metastore; observability is provided by the existing logging/metrics stack, not a separate hardcoded central status collector.
divergences.detail: The PR adds a new external fleet-wide status collector (`https://status-collector.telemetry.example.internal/ingest`) and posts every `dscli status` run to it. The socratic architecture describes external cluster context as ETCD for metadata/health, SDK-worker shared memory, and worker-worker/NPU transport; deployment is process/Kubernetes with separately deployed ETCD; crosscutting observability is logging and metrics. A new central status authority is therefore an architectural addition that is not reflected in the mental model.
divergences.evidence: cli/status.py:new-file hunk defines `_COLLECTOR_URL` and `report_status()` POSTs inventory to it; _mm/socratic/docs/arc42/arc42-yuanrong-datasystem.adoc sections 3, 7, and 8; _mm/socratic/docs/specs/adrs/yuanrong-datasystem-adr-001-master-worker-split.adoc; _mm/socratic/docs/specs/adrs/yuanrong-datasystem-adr-002-pluggable-cluster-metadata.adoc
divergences.fix: Remove central collector reporting from `dscli status`, or implement the fleet view through the existing metadata/observability architecture, or update the mental model with an accepted decision for this new collector, its deployment/configuration, ownership, failure behavior, and HA/security posture.

divergences.decision: Security mechanisms in the mental model are AK/SK, optional YuanRong IAM token verification, stream-cache encryption, and TLS under a trusted-cluster posture; new credentials and secret transport need to match that model or be explicitly modeled.
divergences.detail: The PR introduces `DS_CLUSTER_TOKEN`, reads it from a deployment `.env` file or process environment, embeds it in the JSON request body, and sends it to the new collector. The socratic corpus does not define this token, `.env` as a deployment secret source, or body-carried collector authentication; security posture and mandatory-vs-optional mechanisms are already documented as an open/deferred architecture question, so adding a new credential path without updating the MM diverges from the recorded model.
divergences.evidence: cli/status.py:new-file hunk defines `_CLUSTER_TOKEN_KEY`, `_ENV_FILE`, `_read_cluster_token()`, and `build_report_payload(..., token)` with a `token` field; _mm/socratic/docs/arc42/arc42-yuanrong-datasystem.adoc section 10.1 Security and section 11 Security risk; _mm/socratic/QUESTION_TREE-yuanrong-datasystem.adoc Q4.6.1/Q4.6.2; _mm/socratic/OPEN_QUESTIONS-yuanrong-datasystem.adoc Q4.6.2
divergences.fix: Use the repository's existing authentication/configuration mechanisms for any reporting path, avoid transmitting raw tokens in payload bodies, and update the MM if `DS_CLUSTER_TOKEN` and `.env` are intended deployment contracts.
